outlook.headline: Inventory stays generally healthy, with restocking offsetting isolated build risk
outlook.summary: Inventory conditions look broadly balanced to lean into 2026Q1: customers/distributors are normalizing or restocking at Lam Research, Arrow, GlobalFoundries, Teradyne, Microchip and Renesas, while Micron, Broadcom and MKS point to tight supply. At the same time, a few names are building inventory to secure supply or support growth — notably TSMC, NVIDIA and Applied Materials — so the near-term read is healthier than bloated, but not without pockets of buildup. Watch whether demand stays firm enough to absorb those strategic builds without reviving oversupply concerns.

drivers.label: Lean customer and channel inventories are normalizing
drivers.detail: Multiple reads point to healthy inventory positions rather than broad destocking or glut, supporting supply discipline.

drivers.label: Restocking is underway in select end markets
drivers.detail: Customers are rebuilding inventory after burn-down, which should support shipments over the next 1-2 quarters.

drivers.label: Strategic inventory builds are being used to secure supply
drivers.detail: Higher inventory at several suppliers reflects proactive buffering for demand, not evidence of excess stock.

drivers.label: Supply remains tight in key areas
drivers.detail: Low inventories and tight supply commentary suggest the market is still short in pockets, which keeps the backdrop constructive.

drivers.label: Isolated oversupply pockets still exist
drivers.detail: A few companies flag excess or mixed inventory, limiting the case for a fully bullish read.

risks: Strategic builds at NVIDIA, TSMC or Applied could overshoot if demand softens.
Cabot-like excess inventory ahead of duties or channel distortion could spill into broader pricing pressure.
If restocking stalls, currently lean inventories could quickly revert to destocking behavior.
